right_lyrics:
**"Goodbye to the Hometown Lights"**
*(A Pop Song About Growing Up & Leaving Home)*

**[Verse 1]**
I packed my bags at seventeen,
Dreams too big for this small-town scene.
Mama cried by the old front door,
Said, *"Don’t forget what you’re running for."*

**[Pre-Chorus]**
Yeah, the streets here know my name,
But the world’s got a brighter flame.
I swear I’ll come back someday,
Just not the same.

**[Chorus]**
So I’m waving *goodbye* to the hometown lights,
Chasing the stars through the neon nights.
Every mile feels like a step too far,
But I know who I am—even when I don’t know where I are.
Oh, the road don’t love me yet,
But I’ll learn to love it back—
One last look in the rearview mirror,
Then I’m *gone*.

**[Verse 2]**
Dad said, *"Son, the world’s not kind,"*
But I’ve got fire in my bloodline.
Scraped-up knees and a heart full of fight,
Ready to prove ‘em all right.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
Yeah, the porches still swing slow,
But my soul’s got a faster flow.
I’ll miss the way the river bends,
But I gotta see where it ends.

**[Chorus]**
So I’m waving *goodbye* to the hometown lights,
Chasing the stars through the neon nights.
Every mile feels like a step too far,
But I know who I am—even when I don’t know where I are.
Oh, the road don’t love me yet,
But I’ll learn to love it back—
One last look in the rearview mirror,
Then I’m *gone*.

**[Bridge]**
Maybe I’ll find a place that feels like you,
Or maybe I’ll just outgrow the view.
But the love you gave me’s my compass true—
No matter how far, I’ll carry you.

**[Outro]**
So don’t cry, this ain’t the last time,
I’ll bring you stories from the city skyline.
And when I’m lost, I’ll hear your voice,
Saying, *"Boy, you made the right choice."*

*(Soft guitar fades out…)*

---
**Vibe:** A bittersweet, anthemic pop song with a mix of nostalgia and determination—think *Lewis Capaldi* meets *The 1975* with a dash of *Taylor Swift’s* storytelling. Perfect for a road trip or a tearful airport send-off!

Want any tweaks? More angst? More hope? A key change? 😉

left_lyrics:
**"Goodbye, Hometown Sky"**
*(A bittersweet, anthemic pop song about stepping into the unknown—think the emotional punch of *"Driver’s License"* meets the soaring energy of *"Since U Been Gone."*)*

---

**[Verse 1]**
Packed my bags in the dead of July,
Dust on the floor where the bed used to lie.
Mom left a note by the screen door light:
*"Don’t forget who you are out there tonight."*

**[Verse 2]**
The old oak tree’s still carved with our names,
Backseat kisses and *"Who’s gonna change?"*
But the road’s got a hum like a siren’s call,
And the rearview’s just pixels now—small.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
I swear I won’t cry, but the streetlights blur,
Every turn’s a question, every stop’s a *"What if I—"*
*(What if I fly? What if I fall?)*
*(What if I’m not enough after all?)*

**[Chorus]**
So wave to the past, let the taillights glow,
I’ll miss the way this place used to know my soul.
But the stars out there burn a little brighter,
So I’ll love you from the other side of the sky tonight.
*Goodbye, hometown sky—*
I’m the kid you raised, but I’m not hers to keep.
*Goodbye, one more time—*
The world’s too wide for these small-town streets.

**[Verse 3]**
Dad said, *"Heart’s like a boomerang, kid,"*
*"You throw it out hard, but it always comes back."*
So I’ll leave my key under the mat just in case,
But the lock’s getting rusty from the rain and the wait.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
I swear I won’t cry, but the radio plays
That song from senior year—*god, the way it still stings.*
*(What if I stay? What if I go?)*
*(What if the love was just the not-knowing?)*

**[Chorus]**
So wave to the past, let the taillights glow,
I’ll miss the way this place used to know my soul.
But the stars out there burn a little brighter,
So I’ll love you from the other side of the sky tonight.
*Goodbye, hometown sky—*
I’m the kid you raised, but I’m not hers to keep.
*Goodbye, one more time—*
The world’s too wide for these small-town streets.

**[Bridge]** *(spoken-sung, breathy—builds to climax)*
*"I’ll call when I land,"* that’s what I said,
But the signal cuts out and the voices all blend.
Maybe growing up’s just learning to drive
With the headlights off and the fear alive…

**[Final Chorus]** *(BIG—full belting, gang vocals on the "goodbyes")*
So wave to the past, let the taillights **burn**,
I’ll miss the way this place used to know my **turns**.
But the stars out there **scream** my name louder,
So I’ll love you from the other side of the sky now.
*Goodbye, hometown sky—*
I’m the storm you warned me about in your sleep.
*Goodbye, one last time—*
I’ll send you postcards from the edge of me.

**[Outro]** *(soft, resolute—just vocals + sparse piano/guitar)*
The porch light’s still on,
But I’m gone, I’m gone…
*(I’m gone.)*

---
**Why It Works:**
- **Relatable Core:** Leaving home is universal—nostalgia, fear, and excitement collide.
- **Sing-Along Hooks:** The *"Goodbye, hometown sky"* refrain is simple but devastating. The *"What if I—"* pre-chorus invites crowd shout-backs.
- **Dynamic Arc:** Starts intimate (packing bags), explodes in the chorus, then ends with quiet resolution.
- **Modern Pop Tropes:** *"Taillights glow"* (imagery), *"postcards from the edge of me"* (clever twist on cliché), and the bridge’s *"headlights off"* metaphor for blind faith.
- **Melodic Implication:** Short, punchy lines (*"I’m gone, I’m gone"*) beg for a breathy, syncopated delivery; the chorus’s *"sky tonight"* lends itself to a soaring, sustained note.

**Artist Fit:** This could be a **Taylor Swift** (*"cardigan"* era) or **Olivia Rodrigo** (*"drivers license"* meets *"Brutal"*) smash, or a **Lewis Capaldi**-style power ballad with a pop-punk edge (think **Machine Gun Kelly** meets **The 1975**). Would *kill* with a **finishing drum fill** before the final chorus.

Want a specific artist’s vibe or a different emotional angle? I can tweak it!

left_lyrics:
**"The Words We Never Said"**
*(An Indie Folk Ballad)*

*(Verse 1)*
I saw you standing by the old train tracks,
Half a smile, half a heart attack.
I rehearsed the lines a thousand times before,
But the words just stayed behind the door.

*(Pre-Chorus)*
And the wind carried whispers I couldn’t say,
Like confetti at a parade—
Bright and fleeting, gone too soon,
Lost beneath a harvest moon.

*(Chorus)*
Oh, we had a conversation in my head,
You laughed, I stuttered, the sky turned red.
But the real world’s colder, the real world’s slow,
And the things we mean are the last to go.

*(Verse 2)*
I wrote you letters on napkins at 3 AM,
Folded them up like a bad bet again.
The ink ran blue like the veins in my wrist,
A pulse without a hand to clench into a fist.

*(Pre-Chorus)*
And the radio played our song too loud,
Static hissing through the crowd.
I almost called, but the dial turned,
And the moment burned.

*(Chorus)*
Oh, we had a conversation in my head,
You cried, I held you, the stars bled red.
But the real world’s heavier, the real world’s mean,
And the things we need are the ones unseen.

*(Bridge)*
Maybe in another life, we run into each other,
At a diner where the coffee’s warm and the waitress calls us "honey."
Maybe then I’d say, *"I missed you like hell,"*
Instead of just *"Hey, how’ve you been?"*—so well.

*(Outro)*
Now the tracks are quiet, the trains don’t stop,
And the words we never said just gather like dust on top.
Of all the silences we swore we’d break,
The loudest one’s the love we didn’t take.

---
**Style Notes:**
- **Instrumentation:** Fingerpicked acoustic guitar, soft harmonica, sparse piano, and a distant, echoey drum machine for a lo-fi feel.
- **Vibe:** Think *Phoebe Bridgers* meets *The Weepies*—melancholic but with a glimmer of hope, like sunlight through a half-drawn shade.
- **Delivery:** Whispered verses building to a cracked, emotional chorus.

Want me to tweak the mood or add a specific metaphor? This one’s got *unrequited love* and *regret* baked in, but I can pivot to anger, nostalgia, or even dark humor if you’d like!
right_lyrics:
**"The Porch Light on Maple Drive"**

*(Soft fingerpicked guitar, intimate and hushed, with a slow, swaying rhythm—like a confession at 2 AM.)*

---

**[Verse 1]**
I rehearsed it in the shower steam,
Practiced pauses between the seams.
*"I miss the way you left your keys
In my coat pocket like a promise."*

**[Verse 2]**
The porch light’s been on since July,
A moth’s last dance before it dies.
I could’ve said it when you called at 3:17,
But the stars were loud, and I was scared of *even*.

---

**[Pre-Chorus]**
Now the coffee’s cold, the sugar’s clumped,
And the words I kept are gathering dust.
I should’ve just—

**[Chorus]**
*Told you the truth was a half-packed bag,*
*Your favorite sweater still smelled like campfire ash.*
*I could’ve said, “Stay,” but the freeway hummed,*
*And I let the silence do the talking for us.*

---

**[Verse 3]**
The diner on 5th still plays our song,
A cracked vinyl skipping along.
I trace the cracks in the booth where we sat,
Where you said, *"Maybe next year,"* and that was that.

**[Verse 4]**
I found your note behind the clock,
*"Don’t forget to water the foxglove."*
I did. It died. Just like the chance
To say I loved you more than past tense.

---

**[Bridge]** *(spoken-sung, fragile, over sparse guitar)*
*The thing about unsaid things—*
*They don’t fade. They just*
*Take up space like old winter coats*
*In the back of your throat.*

---

**[Chorus]**
*Now the truth’s a half-packed bag,*
*Your favorite sweater still smells like campfire ash.*
*I should’ve said, “Stay,” but the freeway hummed,*
*And I let the silence do the talking for us.*

**[Outro]** *(fading, like a voice giving out)*
*The porch light’s still on, but you’re long gone,*
*And the conversation’s just the wind,*
*Knocking over chairs we never sat in.*

---
*(Last note lingers, unresolved—like the conversation itself.)*

left_lyrics:
**[Title: "Mirrorball (Dancing Alone)"]**

*(Upbeat, glittery, anthemic pop with a driving bassline and shimmery synths—think Dua Lipa meets early Katy Perry. The lyrics capture the euphoria of self-liberation, with a chorus built for screaming into a hairbrush.)*

---

**[Verse 1]**
*Lock the door, turn the lights down low*
*Speakers scream like they own the show*
*No VIP, just a bed and me*
*But the floor’s a stage when I’m feeling free*

*Left foot stumbles, right foot lies*
*Say I don’t care, but I practice the highs*
*Every spin’s a revolution*
*One-woman riot, no resolution*

**[Pre-Chorus]**
*Oh, they don’t see what I see in the dark*
*A supernova in a hoodie, no spark*
*But the mirror don’t judge when I lose control—*
*I’m the fire, I’m the gold!*

**[Chorus]**
*So let the mirrorball spin, spin, spin!*
*I don’t need a crowd to let me in*
*Every shadow’s a spotlight tonight*
*I’m the love, I’m the fight, I’m the *neon dynamite***
*Oh-oh-oh, watch me glow!*
*No hands to hold, but I don’t move slow*
*If the world won’t dance, then screw the world—*
*I’ll be the girl who *shakes the hurt*!*

**[Verse 2]**
*Mom’s old scarf like a disco ball*
*Tangled up in my *can’t-stop* crawl*
*Lycra dreams and a broken heel*
*But the music’s loud and it’s *how I feel***
*Ex-boyfriend’s voice on the radio*
*I laugh so hard I *almost* let go—*
*Then I turn it up till the walls *crack*
*Yeah, this is my *solo* comeback!

**[Pre-Chorus]**
*Oh, they don’t see what I see in the dark*
*A one-woman show with a *beating* heart*
*But the mirror don’t lie when I steal the show—*
*I’m the queen, now they *know*!*

**[Chorus]**
*So let the mirrorball spin, spin, spin!*
*I don’t need a crown to wear the grin*
*Every shadow’s a spotlight tonight*
*I’m the love, I’m the fight, I’m the *neon dynamite***
*Oh-oh-oh, watch me glow!*
*No hands to hold, but I don’t move slow*
*If the world won’t dance, then screw the world—*
*I’ll be the girl who *shakes the hurt*!*

**[Bridge]** *(spoken-sung, breathy—like a secret)*
*They say, "Who you dancing for?"*
*Like freedom’s a *two-person* sport*
*But the best kind of magic’s the kind you *make*—*
*So I’ll take the *floor* and I’ll take the *ake*…*
*(Beat drop—then explode into final chorus!)*

**[Final Chorus]** *(BIGGER, with ad-libs: "C’mon!", "Work it!", etc.)*
*Let the mirrorball *CRASH*, spin, spin!*
*I’m the afterparty *under my skin*
*Every shadow’s a *spotlight* tonight*
*I’m the *love*, I’m the *fight*—*HEY!*
*Oh-oh-oh, *WATCH ME BURN*!*
*No lessons learned, but I *lost my turn*
*If the world won’t *dance*—*THEN LET IT BURN*!*
*I’m the girl who *shakes the*—*
*(WHISPER:)* ***"Hurt."*** *(then full stop—last beat hits like a punchline.)*

---
**Why It Works:**
- **Relatable Core:** Dancing alone as self-care/empowerment (universal but fresh).
- **Rhythmic Play:** Short, punchy lines ("*No VIP, just a bed and me*") contrast with soaring chorus melodies.
- **Sing-Along Hooks:** "*Neon dynamite*" and "*shakes the hurt*" are designed to be screamed.
- **Bridge Payoff:** The spoken-word moment adds intimacy before the explosive finale.
- **Resolution:** Ends on a whisper—*mic drop*—leaving the listener breathless.

**Artist Vibes:** Doja Cat’s swagger + Robyn’s emotional rawness + the glitter of *Kylie Minogue’s "Padam Padam."* Would kill on TikTok with the mirrorball visuals. 💿✨

right_lyrics:
**"BREAK THE CHAINS"**

*(A rallying cry for the ones who refuse to kneel—raw, defiant, and built for a crowd of raised fists.)*

---

**[Verse 1]**
Neon signs and hollow lies,
They sell the dream but steal your life.
Numbers stamped on factory skin,
A ghost inside a clockwork grin.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
But I hear the crack in the concrete floor,
A whisper turning to a roar—
*"You don’t own us, we’re not yours!"*

**[Chorus]**
**We are the spark in the midnight black,**
**A wildfire burning—no turning back!**
**Tear down the walls, rewrite the rules,**
**We are the storm, we are the proof!**
*(Break the chains! Break the chains!)*

**[Verse 2]**
Suits and ties in ivory towers,
Counting coins while we count hours.
But the streets don’t bow, the streets don’t sleep,
They’re paved with the dreams they couldn’t keep.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
Now the match is lit, the fuse is short,
A revolution’s just a heartbeat’s thought—
*"You built the cage, we’ll bring it down!"*

**[Chorus]**
**We are the spark in the midnight black,**
**A wildfire burning—no turning back!**
**Tear down the walls, rewrite the rules,**
**We are the storm, we are the proof!**
*(Break the chains! Break the chains!)*

**[Bridge]** *(Half-time, spoken-sung, building tension)*
*"They say, 'Stay quiet, take your place,'*
*But the graveyard’s full of the well-behaved.*
*So light it up, let ‘em see—*
*The only hell we know’s the one they made for me!"*

**[Guitar Solo]** *(Screaming, chaotic, then resolving into a driving rhythm—think Slash meets Billy Joe Armstrong.)*

**[Final Chorus]** *(Bigger, grittier, crowd screaming the ad-libs)*
**We are the spark in the midnight black,**
*(HEY!)*
**A wildfire burning—no turning back!**
*(NO!)*
**Tear down the walls, rewrite the rules,**
*(TEAR IT DOWN!)*
**We are the storm, we are the proof!**
*(BREAK THE CHAINS! BREAK THE CHAINS!)*

**[Outro]** *(Slow, defiant, fading with feedback)*
The sun will rise on a different day,
The ashes cold, but we’re here to stay.
*(Break the chains… break the chains…)*

---
**Why it works:**
- **Anthemic chorus** with a call-and-response hook (*"Break the chains!"*).
- **Imagery** of fire, storms, and rebellion (classic rock tropes with a modern edge).
- **Rhythmic contrast**—short, punchy lines in verses vs. soaring chorus.
- **Bridge** adds a moment of raw, spoken defiance before the explosive finale.
- **Guitar solo** placement for maximum impact (post-bridge, pre-climax).

**For the stage:** Crowd claps on the *"HEY!"* and *"NO!"* ad-libs, fists in the air for the final chorus. Encores demand a pyro burst on *"wildfire burning."*

Want it heavier? Add a **pre-verse riff** (à la *"Smells Like Teen Spirit"*). Want it more punk? Shorten the solo, speed up the tempo. This is your rebellion—**own it.**

right_lyrics:
**"NO CHAINS, NO NAME"**

*(A high-octane anthem for the restless—where the highway is the only law and the wind carries the truth.)*

---

**[Verse 1]**
Dust in my teeth and a tank full of sin,
Map’s torn to hell but I’m grinning again.
The rearview’s a ghost, just a flicker of smoke,
Every mile I steal is a lock that I broke.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
Oh, the cops and the preachers can scream all they want,
But the white line’s my Bible and the night is my font.
I ain’t lost—I’m exactly where I need to be:
Nowhere to run and the devil in me.

**[Chorus]**
**No chains, no name, just the roar in my veins!**
The road don’t ask where I came from or why.
I’m the spark in the dark, I’m the howl in the rain,
A backseat full of dreams and a pedal to the sky!
**No chains, no name!**

**[Verse 2]**
Neon signs bleed like a lover’s last lie,
I traded her ring for a tank full of light.
The radio’s static, but it sings just the same:
*"You can’t outrun hell, but you sure can outdrive the pain."*

**[Pre-Chorus]**
Oh, the laws and the borders are written in sand,
But my wheels write the gospel the damned understand.
I ain’t free—I’m just too wild to be caught.
The only jail I know is the one that I’m *not*.

**[Chorus]**
**No chains, no name, just the roar in my veins!**
The road don’t ask where I came from or why.
I’m the spark in the dark, I’m the howl in the rain,
A backseat full of dreams and a pedal to the sky!
**No chains, no name!**

**[Bridge]** *(spoken-sung, half-time groove, then exploding back in)*
*"They’ll tell you to slow down…*
*That the night’s got no mercy…*
*But mercy’s just another word*
*For a cage with a curse on it.*
*So light it up, kid—*
*The only funeral I fear*
*Is the one where my engine don’t scream."*

**[Guitar Solo]** *(A wailing, feedback-drenched sermon—part lament, part war cry.)*

**[Final Chorus]** *(bigger, rawer, gang vocals)*
**NO CHAINS, NO NAME, JUST THE FIRE IN MY BONES!**
The road don’t care if I’m broken or whole.
I’m the flash in the pan, I’m the ghost in the grain,
A matchbook confession and a one-way lane!
**NO CHAINS—** *(hell, no!)*
**NO NAME—** *(just the wind!)*

**[Outro]** *(fading, defiant, engine revs and a final, feedback-laden chord)*
*"Tell ‘em I kept going…*
*Tell ‘em I never stopped…*
*Tell ‘em the road and I—*
*We’re the last two things*
*God couldn’t lock."*

---
**How to perform it:**
- **Verse:** Gritty, half-sung/half-spoken, like a confession at 90 mph.
- **Pre-Chorus:** Build tension with a swaggering, stop-start rhythm.
- **Chorus:** Full-throated, fist-in-the-air—crowd should drown you out by the second repeat.
- **Bridge:** Start like a campfire story, end like a riot.
- **Solo:** Let it *bleed*—this is where the guitar player earns their stripes.
- **Outro:** Leave ‘em with the sound of an engine disappearing into the dark.

**Themes:** Rebellion as religion, motion as medicine, the road as the only true confessional.
**Influences:** Springsteen’s storytelling, AC/DC’s swagger, The Clash’s defiance, and a touch of Lynyrd Skynyrd’s fatalism.

Now go play it loud enough to wake the dead. 🚗💨🔥

theme: the simple life and front porch memories
left_lyrics:
**"Front Porch Gospel"**

*(A slow-burning, 3/4 waltz with a warm, acoustic guitar-driven melody—think "Strawberry Wine" meets "Tennessee Whiskey.")*

---

**[Verse 1]**
Daddy’s old hound dog sleeps in the evening shade,
His tail still thumps when I hum that *Red Wing* tune he made.
Mama’s rockin’ slower now, but her voice still rings,
Like a church bell callin’ all the lost boys in.

**[Verse 2]**
Lightnin’ bugs in mason jars, flickerin’ gold and dim,
We’d chase ‘em down to the creek bank, skinnin’ knees and grinnin’.
Uncle Joe’d tune his fiddle while the coffee perked,
And the whole damn world felt like a song we’d learned.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, it’s the creak of the swing and the screen door’s sigh,
The way the past don’t fade—it just waves goodbye.
Ain’t no sermon sweeter than the truth we’d steal,
Sittin’ there soakin’ up that front porch gospel.

**[Verse 3]**
Now the paint’s peelin’ like the years rolled off too fast,
But I still see your shadow where the porch light cast.
That old glider’s rusted, but it sings your name,
When the south wind blows through like a ghost train.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, it’s the creak of the swing and the screen door’s sigh,
The way the past don’t fade—it just waves goodbye.
Ain’t no sermon sweeter than the truth we’d steal,
Sittin’ there soakin’ up that front porch gospel.

**[Bridge]**
Some say heaven’s a city with the streets of gold,
But I’ll take a splintered plank and a story told.
‘Cause the angels up there ain’t got nothin’ on
The way your laugh still echoes in this old front yard.

**[Outro]**
So I’ll leave the light on, let the crickets preach,
Let the night wind hum what the heart can’t reach.
And if you listen close, you can still hear it all—
The front porch gospel callin’ us back home.

---
**Why It Works:**
- **Imagery:** Lightnin’ bugs in jars, rusted gliders, screen doors—tactile, nostalgic details that feel *lived-in*.
- **Emotional Arc:** Starts warm (childhood), turns bittersweet (loss/time), resolves with quiet hope (memory as comfort).
- **Singability:** Chorus lifts with a soaring, vowel-heavy melody ("*oh-oh-oh*" hooks), while verses flow like conversation.
- **Universal Theme:** "Home" as both a place and a feeling, with a touch of spiritual metaphor (porch as church, memories as gospel).

**Artist Comp:** Imagine *Tyler Childers*’ raw storytelling meets *Trisha Yearwood*’s aching delivery, with a dash of *Jason Isbell*’s poetic grit.

Want a twist? Swap the bridge for a **harmonica solo** or add a **call-and-response** ("*Can I get an amen?*" / "*Amen*") to lean into the "gospel" theme.

left_lyrics:
**"3 AM Sidewalk Serenade"**
*(An Indie Song About Walking Through the City at 3 AM)*

**[Verse 1]**
Neon signs hum a lullaby,
Streetlights flicker like tired eyes.
The city’s breath is slow and low,
Just me and the ghosts I know.

Pavement cracks like old love letters,
Echoes bounce off empty betters.
A taxi hums a yellow tune,
But I’m not ready to go home soon.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
Oh, the night’s a thief,
Steals the weight right out my teeth.
Every step’s a metronome,
Tick-tock, but I’m not alone.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, 3 AM, you’re my old friend,
Wrapping ‘round me like the wind.
The world’s asleep, but I’m wide awake,
Dancing with the mistakes I make.
The stars are just pinholes in the black,
But I don’t wanna take it back—
No, I don’t wanna take it back.

**[Verse 2]**
A diner glows like a half-remembered dream,
Coffee stains and cigarette steam.
The waitress smiles, but she don’t ask,
Just fills my cup to the brim at last.

Somewhere a siren sings a blues,
A lover’s quarrel in cheap perfume.
I light a match, watch the flame bend,
Then let the dark take it again.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
Oh, the night’s a thief,
Steals the weight right out my teeth.
Every step’s a metronome,
Tick-tock, but I’m not alone.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, 3 AM, you’re my old friend,
Wrapping ‘round me like the wind.
The world’s asleep, but I’m wide awake,
Dancing with the mistakes I make.
The stars are just pinholes in the black,
But I don’t wanna take it back—
No, I don’t wanna take it back.

**[Bridge]**
Maybe I’m just a shadow now,
A silhouette the moon allows.
Maybe the dawn will wash me clean,
Or maybe I’ll just disappear
Into the static, into the glow,
Another ghost the city knows.

**[Outro]**
So let the sun take its sweet time,
I’ll walk these streets like a half-written rhyme.
The night’s my church, the sidewalk’s my pew,
And I ain’t done saying my goodbyes to you.

*(Soft, fading guitar—just a few lingering notes, like footsteps disappearing down an empty street.)*

---
**Vibe:** Think *The War on Drugs* meets *Phoebe Bridgers*—warm, reverb-drenched guitars, a steady drum machine pulse, and a voice that’s half-whisper, half-sigh. Perfect for a lone walk under flickering streetlights.
right_lyrics:
**"3AM on Division Street"**

*(A song about insomnia, memory, and the way cities feel like old friends at night.)*

---

**[Verse 1]**
Streetlamp flickers like a dying TV,
Your voice still hums in the static for me.
The diner’s closed but the neon’s still on,
A half-lit sign singing *"Come home, come home."*

**[Verse 2]**
I trace the cracks in the sidewalk like veins,
Past the laundromat’s slow-motion rain.
The bus stop bench where you tied your shoe,
Now just holds a guy who looks like you.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
And the city exhales through the alleyway vents,
A warm, sick breath of cigarettes and lent.
I could call, but what’s left to say?
The night’s got a way of eating the day.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, Division Street’s a graveyard of light,
Where the ghosts all glow but they don’t shine so bright.
And I’m just a shadow with a heart full of noise,
Another lost boy with a pocket of coins.

**[Verse 3]**
The bodega clerk counts his change,
His radio plays our old song—so strange.
I buy a coffee I won’t drink,
Just to feel something sink.

**[Bridge]**
The L train rumbles like a threat or a prayer,
Shaking the bones of the buildings up there.
I swear I see you in the backseat of cars,
But it’s just the dark playing tricks with its scars.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, Division Street’s a graveyard of light,
Where the ghosts all glow but they don’t shine so bright.
And I’m just a shadow with a heart full of noise,
Another lost boy with a pocket of coins.

**[Outro]**
The sky’s turning blue like a bad tattoo,
Dawn’s got a way of making it true.
I’ll sleep when the sun steals the street,
But the night always keeps what it needs.

left_lyrics:
**"The Way the Light Left Your Hands"**

*(A song about the quiet unraveling of something precious—how the smallest things become landmarks of loss.)*

---

**[Verse 1]**
Diner at 3 AM on Route 17,
You stirred your coffee like it owed you something.
The waitress called you "hon" like she knew you better
Than I did by then—maybe she did, I don’t know.

**[Verse 2]**
Your keys on the counter, the way they jingled
Like a confession you couldn’t quite swallow.
I counted the cracks in the ceiling tiles,
Prayed to a god made of fluorescent and static.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
And you said, *"It’s not you,"* like a bad movie line,
But your voice was a house with the lights turned out.
I should’ve known by the way you held your cigarettes—
Like something you loved but were done with.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, these are the saints of my personal hell:
The way the light left your hands when you told me,
The clink of your fork on a half-eaten plate,
The radio playing our song in the parking lot.
I’d trade every *"I love you"* for the silence before it,
For the way you almost reached for me—*almost*.

**[Verse 3]**
Your hoodie still smells like your laundry detergent,
Some cheap drugstore brand, something with *"ocean"* in the name.
I wear it to sleep like a fool, like a penance,
Like if I drown in it, I’ll wake up where you are.

**[Bridge]**
And I know, I know—
Love’s just a word for the way we misplace our keys,
For the way we swear we’ll remember the grocery list,
For the way we promise the dog we’ll be back soon.
But the dog always knows first.
The dog always knows.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, these are the saints of my personal hell:
The way the light left your hands when you told me,
The clink of your fork on a half-eaten plate,
The radio playing our song in the parking lot.
I’d trade every *"I love you"* for the silence before it,
For the way you almost reached for me—*almost*.

**[Outro]**
Now the diner’s a bank with a drive-thru ATM,
And the waitress don’t work there no more.
But sometimes I still park in the lot after midnight,
Just to sit in the glow of the sign like a fool.
Like a fool.
Like a fool.

---

**Notes on the song:**
- **Specificity:** Route 17, a diner-turned-bank, a *"cheap drugstore brand"*—these ground the song in a tangible world.
- **Unexpected metaphors:** Love as *"a word for the way we misplace our keys"* twists the mundane into the profound.
- **Emotional devastation in quiet details:** The *"clink of your fork,"* the *"radio playing our song in the parking lot"*—these are the moments that linger, not the big fights or declarations.
- **Rhyme scheme:** Mostly slant rhymes (e.g., *"better"/"know,"* *"penance"/"where you are"*) for a natural, conversational feel.
- **Structure:** Builds from scene-setting verses to a chorus that lands like a gut-punch, with the bridge offering a moment of philosophical resignation before the final chorus and outro.

Would fit nicely on a late-night drive playlist, ideally played while staring out the window at passing streetlights.
right_lyrics:
**"Paperclips & Porch Lights"**
*(An Indie Song About the Small Things That Hold the World Together)*

*(Verse 1)*
You left your coffee cup on my nightstand again,
Half-drunk, cold, with a lipstick stain like a secret you penned.
I trace the rim with my thumb like it’s Braille for the heart,
A language we speak when the big words fall apart.

*(Pre-Chorus)*
And the way that you laugh when the toast burns black,
Or the static hum of the radio playing back—
Some song we don’t know but we sing it anyway,
Like we’re two lost ships with the same broken compass.

*(Chorus)*
Oh, it’s the paperclips bent into hearts on my desk,
The way that you steal all the blankets but leave me the rest.
It’s the porch light you leave on when I’m running late,
The crumbs in the butter, the dent in my side of the bed—
These are the constellations I navigate by,
Tiny infinities tucked in the corner of my eye.

*(Verse 2)*
Your hand finds mine when the movie gets sad,
Not to fix it, just to say, *"Yeah, me too—it’s bad."*
The grocery list scribbled with your left-handed slant,
*"Eggs, milk, and that weird hot sauce you can’t live without (but I can)."*

*(Pre-Chorus)*
And the way that you sigh when the wind chimes fight,
Or the dust on the records we swear we’ll play tonight—
We’re collectors of moments too fragile to name,
Like dandelion wishes or matches that won’t flame.

*(Chorus)*
Oh, it’s the paperclips bent into hearts on my desk,
The way that you steal all the blankets but leave me the rest.
It’s the porch light you leave on when I’m running late,
The crumbs in the butter, the dent in my side of the bed—
These are the constellations I navigate by,
Tiny infinities tucked in the corner of my eye.

*(Bridge)*
Maybe love isn’t fireworks or grand gestures loud,
Maybe it’s the silence when the whole world’s too proud.
Maybe it’s the way that your keys sound in the lock,
The way that my soul says *"Oh, there you are"* when you walk.

*(Outro)*
So I’ll keep your lost earring in my junk drawer like gold,
And the ticket stubs from the movie we slept through, half-folded and old.
‘Cause the universe writes its love letters small,
In the cracks of the sidewalk, the echoes down the hall.

*(Soft, fading)*
Oh, it’s the paperclips… it’s the porch light…
It’s you, in the quiet, making my whole life bright.

---
**Style Notes:**
- **Instrumentation:** Warm acoustic guitar, soft electric leads, subtle synth pads, and a steady kick drum with brushed snare. Think *Phoebe Bridgers* meets *The Weepies* with a dash of *Big Thief’s* intimate storytelling.
- **Delivery:** Breathy, conversational verses building to a soaring but still tender chorus. The bridge should feel like a whispered secret.
- **Vibe:** Nostalgic but present, bittersweet but hopeful—like watching fireflies at dusk.

Would love to hear how you’d tweak it! Any lines hit home? 💛

left_lyrics:
**"Dance Floor (My Bedroom)"**
*(A Pop Anthem for Solo Groovers)*

**[Verse 1]**
*Mirror on the wall, yeah, you’re my only crowd,*
*Spotlight’s just my lamp turned up way too loud.*
*No DJ, no bass drop, but the beat’s in my soul,*
*I don’t need a club when I’ve got this much control.*

**[Pre-Chorus]**
*Left foot, right foot, spin until I fall,*
*No one’s watchin’—so I’ll just own it all.*
*From the ceiling fan light to the rug on the floor,*
*This whole room’s a stage, yeah, I’m askin’ for more!*

**[Chorus]**
*So I’ll dance, dance, dance like nobody’s home,*
*Sing off-key to the songs in my phone.*
*No judgment, no rules, just me and the night,*
*My bedroom’s the best damn club in town tonight!*
*Oh-oh-oh, I don’t need a partner,*
*Oh-oh-oh, I’m the life of the party (hey!).*
*Spin me ‘round, let the music take hold—*
*Best night ever, and I’m flyin’ solo!*

**[Verse 2]**
*My hairbrush mic’s got a Grammy-worthy sound,*
*And this pillow fort’s the VIP lounge.*
*No last call, no cover, just my heart in the speakers,*
*Dancin’ ‘til the sun comes up—call me a weekend seeker.*

**[Pre-Chorus]**
*Left foot, right foot, spin until I fall,*
*No one’s watchin’—so I’ll just own it all.*
*From the laundry-pile mountains to the posters I’ve hung,*
*This whole room’s alive with the songs I’ve just sung!*

**[Chorus]**
*So I’ll dance, dance, dance like nobody’s home,*
*Sing off-key to the songs in my phone.*
*No judgment, no rules, just me and the night,*
*My bedroom’s the best damn club in town tonight!*
*Oh-oh-oh, I don’t need a partner,*
*Oh-oh-oh, I’m the life of the party (hey!).*
*Spin me ‘round, let the music take hold—*
*Best night ever, and I’m flyin’ solo!*

**[Bridge]**
*Maybe one day I’ll hit the real dance floor,*
*But tonight? Nah, I’m slayin’ right here on my rug.*
*No FOMO, no stress, just me in my glow,*
*‘Cause the best kind of magic’s the kind that you know.*

**[Outro]**
*So turn it up, let the neighbors complain,*
*I’ll be too busy losin’ my mind in the rain (of glitter I spilled).*
*One more spin, then I’ll crash like a star—*
*But tonight? This bedroom’s *ours* (yeah, mine—*duh*).*

---
**Vibe:** Upbeat, glittery, and unapologetically joyful—think Dua Lipa meets early Katy Perry with a dash of bedroom-pop intimacy. Perfect for belting into a hairbrush at 1 AM!

Want a specific melody or genre twist? I can tweak it! 🎶✨

right_lyrics:
**"Gone Before the Sunset"**
*(A Country Ballad of Love and Loss)*

*(Verse 1)*
The preacher said his prayers real slow,
While the rain tapped on the church window.
I held your mama’s hand so tight,
But nothin’ could make this feel right.

*(Verse 2)*
Still got your coat hangin’ by the door,
Your coffee cup sittin’ where you left it on the floor.
The bed’s too wide, the nights too long,
And the silence sings our favorite song.

*(Chorus)*
Oh, you were gone before the sunset,
Like a dream I ain’t woke up from yet.
God must’ve needed an angel bad,
‘Cause He took the best one I ever had.
Now I’m just a shadow where you used to be,
A half-written love story with a missin’ page to me.

*(Verse 3)*
I drive by the old oak tree,
Where you carved our names for the world to see.
Now the letters fade like the memories do,
And I wonder if heaven’s as blue as you.

*(Verse 4)*
The dogs still wait by the screen door swing,
Like they’re hopin’ you’ll walk in.
I tell ‘em, "Boys, she ain’t comin’ home,"
But I ain’t sure I believe it myself some nights alone.

*(Chorus)*
Oh, you were gone before the sunset,
Like a fire that burned out before the embers met.
I’d give every breath I got left to take,
Just to hear you laugh one more time, for heaven’s sake.
Now I’m just a ghost in a house too quiet,
A man who’s learnin’ how to live without your light.

*(Bridge)*
They say time’s s’posed to heal the hurt,
But time just teaches me what I’m worth—
A love that deep don’t just fade away,
It haunts you sweet in the light of day.

*(Outro)*
So I’ll keep your picture by the bed,
And talk to you like you ain’t dead.
And when my time comes, darlin’, don’t you cry,
‘Cause I’ll be holdin’ you again beneath that sweet bye-and-bye.

*(Soft guitar fade-out...)*

---
This song blends classic country themes—heartache, faith, and everyday details that make loss feel real. If you'd like any tweaks (more gospel influence, a specific memory, etc.), let me know. Hope it resonates. 🎶💔

left_lyrics:
**"Dirt Road Lullabies"**
*(A Country Song About Raisin’ Kids in the Country)*

*(Verse 1)*
Well, the sun comes up on a screen door swayin’,
Coffee’s black and the bacon’s frying.
Little boots hit the porch with a clatter,
Muddy hands and a "Daddy, what’s the matter?"

I say, "Son, that’s just the way it goes,
Where the creek runs deep and the wild wind blows.
Ain’t no sidewalk, just a gravel lane,
But these old dirt roads know your name."

*(Chorus)*
Oh, we’re raisin’ dreams where the corn grows high,
Where the fireflies dance in a July sky.
Skinned-up knees and a mama’s prayer,
Lord, keep ‘em safe out there.
From the tractor rides to the bonfire light,
Every scar’s a story, every star’s just right.
Ain’t no place I’d rather be,
Than singin’ dirt road lullabies to you and me.

*(Verse 2)*
She’s eight years old with a fishin’ pole,
Sittin’ patient where the catfish roll.
Says, "Daddy, why’s the sky so blue?"
I say, "Baby, that’s God shinin’ through."

We got a dog named Bo and a barn full of grace,
A swing on the oak where the time stands still in this place.
Ain’t no rush hour, just the cows comin’ home,
And the sound of your laugh like a song I’ve always known.

*(Chorus)*
Oh, we’re raisin’ dreams where the corn grows high,
Where the fireflies dance in a July sky.
Skinned-up knees and a mama’s prayer,
Lord, keep ‘em safe out there.
From the tractor rides to the bonfire light,
Every scar’s a story, every star’s just right.
Ain’t no place I’d rather be,
Than singin’ dirt road lullabies to you and me.

*(Bridge)*
Someday they’ll drive down a two-lane blacktop,
Chasin’ dreams in a world that don’t stop.
But I hope they remember the things that stay true—
The smell of the rain and the work of your hands,
And the love that’s planted deep where the old home stands.

*(Outro)*
So here’s to the kids with the wild in their eyes,
Who know every trail where the rabbit hides.
To the prayers whispered over scraped-up knees,
And the way this old land bends the breeze.
Yeah, the world may turn, but we’ll hold it tight,
Right here where the porch light burns all night.
Just you, me, and these dirt road lullabies.

*(Soft guitar fade-out...)*

---
Hope you like it! This song captures the heart of raising kids where the land teaches ‘em more than any book could. Perfect for a campfire or a slow drive down a backroad. 🎸🌾

right_lyrics:
**"Ocean Between Us"**
*(A Pop Ballad About Longing & Distance)*

**[Verse 1]**
I trace your name on my windowpane,
The rain just falls like tears again.
Your voice still plays on my old voicemail,
But it’s just static now—my heart’s a sail,
Lost in the waves between us.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
I swear I see you in the city lights,
A shadow moves just like your stride.
But when I blink, you disappear,
And I’m left here—
Wishing you were near.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, there’s an ocean between us,
A thousand miles of blue.
Every night I’m reaching,
But my hands can’t hold you.
Time zones keep us silent,
The sunrise steals my view.
If love’s a bridge, then why does it feel
Like I’m drowning in the space between me and you?

**[Verse 2]**
Your hoodie still smells like your cologne,
I wear it when I’m missing home.
The stars don’t shine the same alone,
And my bed’s too wide, my heart’s a phone—
Always on, but no reply.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
I count the days on my calendar,
Each one’s a scar that won’t heal over.
If I could fold the map in half,
I’d run so fast—
Just to crash into your arms.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, there’s an ocean between us,
A thousand miles of blue.
Every night I’m reaching,
But my hands can’t hold you.
Time zones keep us silent,
The sunrise steals my view.
If love’s a bridge, then why does it feel
Like I’m drowning in the space between me and you?

**[Bridge]**
Maybe one day, the tides will turn,
And the miles will finally burn.
Till then, I’ll send my love in waves,
Hoping you’re still brave enough to stay…
(Stay…)

**[Outro]**
Oh, there’s an ocean between us,
But I’m swimming through.
Even if the current pulls me under,
I’ll find my way back to you.
Yeah, there’s an ocean between us—
But darling, I’m coming home to you.

---
**Vibe:** Think *The Chainsmokers meets Lewis Capaldi*—a mix of electronic-pop production with raw, emotional vocals. The bridge could build with a soaring synth or a simple, stripped-down piano for maximum feels.

Want a specific melody or artist reference to match? 😊

right_lyrics:
**"THE LONG WAY BACK"**

*(A rock anthem for the ones who carry the weight—seen and unseen.)*

---

**[Verse 1]**
Dust on my boots, a ghost in the hall,
Echoes of orders, but the war’s not here at all.
The porch light’s still burning, yellow and weak,
Like the sun through the smoke where the sand turned to heat.

**[Verse 2]**
They slap my back, call me "hero," pour me a drink,
But the faces I lost are the ones that won’t blink.
The TV’s too loud, the room’s spinning slow,
I left half my soul where the mortars still go.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
Yeah, they painted a line, said, "Boy, stay behind,"
But the line got blurred with the blood and the grime.
Now the only thing steady’s the shake in my hands,
And the weight of the words that no one understands.

**[Chorus]**
I took the long way back, but I’m still not home,
The roads all look different when you’re walking alone.
They gave me a medal, but they can’t give me back
The man I was before I went down that track.
*Oh, I’m here—but I’m not.*
*I’m alive—but I’m not.*

**[Verse 3]**
The dog don’t remember, just licks my old scars,
While the neighbors all whisper ‘bout the man in the car.
I see ‘em at night in the headlights’ glow,
The ones who didn’t make it—*they’re the ones that I know.*

**[Pre-Chorus]**
Yeah, they taught me to fight, but they never said how
To unload this rifle when the war’s over now.
And the stars up above just look cold as the grave,
Like the eyes of the men that I couldn’t save.

**[Chorus]**
I took the long way back, but I’m still not home,
The roads all look different when you’re walking alone.
They gave me a medal, but they can’t give me back
The man I was before I went down that track.
*Oh, I’m here—but I’m not.*
*I’m alive—but I’m not.*

**[Bridge]** *(spoken/sung, raw and quiet at first, then building)*
*They’ll tell you it’s glory.*
*They’ll tell you it’s right.*
*But the cost ain’t in dollars—*
*It’s paid every night.*
*With the sweat on your sheets*
*And the scream in your dreams,*
*And the part of your heart*
*That’s still stuck overseas.*

**[Guitar Solo]** *(mournful, bluesy, then exploding into a wailing, feedback-drenched climax—think Slash meets Tom Morello.)*

**[Final Chorus]** *(bigger, angrier, defiant—crowd screams the italicized lines)*
I took the long way back, but I’M **STILL NOT HOME**,
The roads all look different when you’re **WALKING ALONE**.
They gave me a medal, but they **CAN’T GIVE ME BACK**
The man I was before I went **DOWN THAT TRACK**.
*Oh, I’m here—but I’m NOT.*
*I’m alive—but I’m NOT.*
*I’m here—but I’M FUCKING NOT.*

**[Outro]** *(slow, heavy, resolved—like a soldier’s exhausted march)*
The screen door creaks shut, the night’s turning black,
The stars look the same… but they don’t bring him back.
No, they don’t bring him back.
*(No, they don’t bring him back.)*

---
**Notes for Performance:**
- **Verse Delivery:** Gritty, half-sung/half-spoken—like Springsteen’s *"Born in the U.S.A."* meets Eddie Vedder’s *"Alive."*
- **Chorus:** Anthemic, fist-in-the-air—imagine a crowd of vets and their families screaming *"I’m here—but I’m NOT."*
- **Bridge:** Start stripped-down (acoustic, vocal crack), then explode into distorted chaos before the solo.
- **Solo:** Should feel like a **release**—start sorrowful, end in defiant screams of feedback.
- **Outro:** Let the last line hang in the air like a question no one can answer.

**Themes:** Survival guilt, the invisibility of PTSD, the lie of "coming home" when the war stays with you. **Not a political song—it’s a human one.**

Would sound massive live with a **wall of Marshall stacks** and a **choir of backing vocals** on the chorus. Light it with **flickering orange/red** (like desert sunsets or distant fires).

Want it heavier? Add a **drop-tuned riff** under the chorus. Want it rawer? Strip it to **acoustic + harmonica** for a Dylan-esque protest feel. Either way—**it hits like a gut punch.**

left_lyrics:
**"Neon Dreams"**
*(A Pop Anthem for the Chasers)*

**[Verse 1]**
Midnight train, one-way ticket in my hand,
Heart’s a drum, yeah, I’m ready for the plan.
Skyscrapers kiss the stars like old friends,
I don’t know ‘em yet, but I will by the weekend.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
They say it’s lonely at the top, well, let’s see,
I’ll take the long nights if it’s calling me.
Every "no" just fuels the fire in my veins,
I came to turn these city lights to *my* name.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, I’m running through the neon dreams,
Dodging shadows, chasing screams.
Every street’s a stage, every doubt’s a lie,
I’ll be the spark that lights the sky—
**Tonight!** (Hey!)
I’ll make ‘em remember who I am,
Lost the map but I’ve got a plan.
Big city heart with a small-town fight,
I’m gonna burn so *damn* bright!

**[Verse 2]**
Subway hums like a lullaby,
Singing, *"Girl, you’re gonna fly."*
Waitress tips and a guitar case,
But I swear this grind’s got a taste—
Like gold on my tongue, like the crowd’s in my veins,
One more shot, one more *yes*, one more name.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
They say it’s lonely at the top, well, let’s see,
I’ll take the long nights if it’s calling me.
Every scar’s just proof I’m still alive,
I came to make this city *mine*.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, I’m running through the neon dreams,
Dodging shadows, chasing screams.
Every street’s a stage, every doubt’s a lie,
I’ll be the spark that lights the sky—
**Tonight!** (Hey!)
I’ll make ‘em remember who I am,
Lost the map but I’ve got a plan.
Big city heart with a small-town fight,
I’m gonna burn so *damn* bright!

**[Bridge]**
(Oh-oh-oh) Hear the sirens sing my song,
(Oh-oh-oh) Even when the nights are long.
I’ll dance with the ghosts of the ones who tried,
But I’m the one who’s *still alive*!

**[Outro]**
So let the lights get lost in my eyes,
Let the critics count my highs.
I’ll be the echo in the canyon sound,
The kid who never *backed down*.

*(Fade out with a pulsing synth, distant city sounds, and a final whispered:)*
**"Neon dreams… don’t sleep."**

---
**Vibe:** Think Dua Lipa’s *"Don’t Start Now"* meets early Katy Perry’s *"Firework"*—glittery, defiant, and made for singing into a hairbrush in front of a skyline view. 🎤✨

Want a specific twist? More rock? More R&B? A rap verse? Let me know!

left_lyrics:
**"3AM on Division Street"**

*(A song about insomnia, memory, and the way cities feel like old friends at night.)*

---

**[Verse 1]**
Streetlamp flickers like a dying TV,
Your voice still hums in the static for me.
The diner’s closed but the neon’s still on,
A half-lit sign singing *"Come home, come home."*

**[Verse 2]**
I trace the cracks in the sidewalk like veins,
Past the laundromat’s slow-motion rain.
The bus stop bench where you tied your shoe,
Now just holds a guy who looks like you.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
And the city exhales through the alleyway vents,
A warm, sick breath of cigarettes and lent.
I could call, but what’s left to say?
The night’s got a way of eating the day.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, Division Street’s a graveyard of light,
Where the ghosts all glow but they don’t shine so bright.
And I’m just a shadow with a heart full of noise,
Another lost boy with a pocket of coins.

**[Verse 3]**
The bodega clerk counts his change,
His radio plays our old song—so strange.
I buy a coffee I won’t drink,
Just to feel something sink.

**[Bridge]**
The L train rumbles like a threat or a prayer,
Shaking the bones of the buildings up there.
I swear I see you in the backseat of cars,
But it’s just the dark playing tricks with its scars.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, Division Street’s a graveyard of light,
Where the ghosts all glow but they don’t shine so bright.
And I’m just a shadow with a heart full of noise,
Another lost boy with a pocket of coins.

**[Outro]**
The sky’s turning blue like a bad tattoo,
Dawn’s got a way of making it true.
I’ll sleep when the sun steals the street,
But the night always keeps what it needs.

right_lyrics:
**"BACK IN THE FIRE"**

*(A storm-chasing anthem for the ghosts of your reckless youth)*

---

**[Verse 1]**
Neon signs and a jukebox scream,
Dime-store whiskey, teenage dreams.
We were lightning in a bottle’s grip,
One-match spark to the whole damn ship.

**[Verse 2]**
Cigarette burns on the dashboard glow,
Radio static singing low.
*"We’ll never die, we’ll just fade away—"*
Liar’s promise, but we believed it anyway.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
Oh, the cops knew our names,
And the bartenders too.
We were kings with no crowns,
Just a hellraising crew.

**[Chorus]**
**We were the fire, we were the flood!**
**Young and stupid, half in love.**
**Now the embers cool, but I swear it’s true—**
**I’d trade it all to burn with you.**
*(Burn with you!)*

**[Verse 3]**
Backseat confessions in the pouring rain,
Your laugh like a bullet through my veins.
We swore we’d never slow down, never quit—
Funny how time steals the things you don’t miss ’til it’s—

**[Pre-Chorus]**
Gone like the smoke from a stolen spark,
Gone like the dark when the streetlights start.
We were ghosts before we knew,
Now I’m just haunting the things we blew through.

**[Chorus]**
**We were the fire, we were the flood!**
**Young and stupid, half in love.**
**Now the embers cool, but I swear it’s true—**
**I’d trade it all to burn with you.**
*(Burn with you!)*

**[Bridge]** *(spoken-sung, half-screamed)*
*"They say you can’t go home—*
*Well, screw ‘em, I’m trying.*
*I’d sell my soul for one more night*
*Where the cops don’t come*
*And the whiskey’s right.*
*Where the world was ours*
*And the rules were lies—*
*God, I’d give anything*
*To see that light in your eyes..."*

**[Final Chorus]** *(bigger, grittier, gang vocals)*
**WE WERE THE FIRE, WE WERE THE FLOOD!**
**BLOOD AND GASOLINE AND MUD!**
**NOW THE DEVIL GRINS LIKE HE KNOWS IT’S TRUE—**
**THE BEST OF US DIED BACK IN ’02.**
*(Back in ’02...)*

**[Outro]** *(slow, defiant, fading)*
Yeah, the scars are still there,
Beneath the suit and the desk chair.
But I hear the sirens call sometimes...
And I *swear* I still smell the smoke in the pines.

---
**How to perform it:**
- **Verses:** Gritty, half-spoken, like a barroom confession. Think Springsteen meets early Gaslight Anthem.
- **Pre-Chorus:** Build tension—harmonies or a dirty guitar slide. The crowd should lean in.
- **Chorus:** Full-throated, fist-in-the-air scream-along. Big drums, bigger guitars. The *"Burn with you!"* line is the sing-it-back moment.
- **Bridge:** Start quiet, then explode into the final chorus. The spoken part should feel like a drunk toast at 3 AM.
- **Outro:** Let the last line hang like a question—*"Do you still hear it too?"*

**Themes:** Nostalgia as a drug, the myth of invincibility, the bittersweet sting of *"we were kings once."* Perfect for a smoky dive bar or a stadium full of people who used to sneak into shows.

Want it meaner? Swap the bridge for a solo that sounds like a car crash. Want it sadder? Slow the outro to a crawl. Either way—**light the match.**

left_lyrics:
**"Better Without You"**
*(A Pop Anthem for Moving On)*

**[Verse 1]**
I kept your hoodie like a fool,
Wore it every night just to feel you.
But the scent’s all gone, now it’s just thread,
Guess my heart finally got the memo instead.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
I cried a river, then I built a boat,
Sailed right past the love you wrote.
Now the waves are calm, and the sky’s so blue—
Funny how the world don’t stop for you.

**[Chorus]**
Oh-oh-oh, I’m better without you,
Dancing in the rain like my heart’s brand new.
Oh-oh-oh, no more late-night "I miss yous,"
Found a love inside me that don’t need you.
Yeah, I’m golden in the rearview,
Watch me glow while you fade to black and blue.
Oh-oh-oh, I’m better without you.

**[Verse 2]**
You said forever like it was a game,
Left me holding all the blame.
But the cracks you made just let the light in,
Now I’m shining like a diamond in the wind.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
I burned the letters, kept the lessons tight,
Turned all your ghosts into firelight.
Now the past’s a song I can skip right through—
Funny how the best revenge is *me* without you.

**[Chorus]**
Oh-oh-oh, I’m better without you,
Laughing at the thought of the love we blew.
Oh-oh-oh, no more "baby, please don’ts,"
I’m the queen of my castle, you’re just the ghost that’s gone.
Yeah, I’m magic in the aftermath,
Watch me rise from the love we crashed.
Oh-oh-oh, I’m better without you.

**[Bridge]**
*Ooh, I see it now—*
You were just a chapter, not the whole damn book.
Took the pain you gave me, turned it into hooks.
Now the crowd sings louder every time I look…
*And you’re just a name I don’t recognize.*

**[Outro]**
Oh-oh-oh, the sun’s still rising,
Even when you’re not in my sky.
Oh-oh-oh, I’m better—*surprise*.
(Yeah, I’m better without you.)

---
**Vibe:** Uplifting, sassy, and cathartic—think Dua Lipa meets Olivia Rodrigo with a glittery revenge twist. Perfect for belting in the car with the windows down! 💔✨

Want a specific melody style or more edge? I can tweak it!

right_lyrics:
**"Walls Start Talking"**
*(A Rock Song About Losing Your Mind in Isolation)*

**[Verse 1]**
Four white walls and a flickering screen,
Echoes hum where my thoughts used to be.
The clock don’t tick, it just laughs in my face,
Time’s a thief, but it won’t leave this place.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
I swear the shadows just whispered my name,
Or was that the wind through the vents again?
The mirror’s a liar, it don’t show me right—
Just a ghost in the glass, fading outta sight.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, the walls start talking when the lights burn low,
Voices in the static, things I used to know.
I’m losing my mind, but who’s there to see?
Just me and the madness—it’s all I can be!

**[Verse 2]**
I count the cracks in the ceiling at night,
Draw constellations from the stains in the light.
The fridge hums hymns in a language I knew,
But the words twist and turn—nothing’s coming through.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
I called my own number just to hear a voice,
But the line went dead when I made my choice.
Now the silence is screaming, it’s loud as a train,
Rattling my ribs like a freight full of pain.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, the walls start talking when the lights burn low,
Phantoms in the corner, things I used to know.
I’m losing my mind, but who’s there to see?
Just me and the madness—it’s all I can be!

**[Bridge]**
*(Guitar solo—wailing, dissonant, like a voice unraveling)*

**[Outro]**
One more day, or a thousand the same,
The calendar’s bleeding, but I don’t feel the pain.
If I scream will it echo? Or swallow me whole?
Guess I’ll find out… when I lose control.

*(Final chord rings out, then cuts abruptly—like a door slamming shut.)*

---
**Style Notes:**
- **Sound:** Think early Alice in Chains meets Nine Inch Nails’ *The Fragile*—heavy, atmospheric, with a creeping sense of paranoia.
- **Delivery:** Start restrained (verse 1), build to a snarling chorus, then let the bridge/solo spiral into chaos before the hollow outro.
- **Bonus:** Add a hidden track at the end—just 10 seconds of distorted, reversed audio whispering *"You were never alone."*

Want it darker? Swap the bridge for a spoken-word breakdown (*"They say solitude’s peace… but peace don’t stare back from the glass…"*). Or go full doom metal with sludgy, detuned riffs. Your call—madness is flexible.

theme: the quiet beauty of ordinary life
left_lyrics:
**"The Way the Light Comes Through"**

*(A song about finding grace in the unremarkable—morning coffee, half-read books, the way dust floats in a sunbeam.)*

---

**[Verse 1]**
6:17, the kettle’s scream
Cuts through the hum of the refrigerator’s dream
Your mug’s got a chip like a crescent moon
And the sugar bowl’s empty, but I’ll spoon some in soon

**[Verse 2]**
The *Times* is still folded to yesterday’s news
A headline about floods we won’t ever use
But the way that you sigh when you turn the page
Is the only thing worth saving in this whole damn age

**[Pre-Chorus]**
Oh, the world keeps ending on a loop
But here in the kitchen, it’s just soup
And the steam from the bowl makes a ghost of your face
For a second, I can’t place

**[Chorus]**
Oh, but this is the gospel, the only truth I know:
Not the fireworks, but the slow, slow glow
The way the light comes through the blinds at noon
The way you say my name when you’re half-asleep in June

**[Verse 3]**
The laundry’s still damp on the radiator
Your sweater smells like rain and a movie theater
We lost the remote under the couch again
And the static on the TV’s our only amen

**[Bridge]**
And I know, I know—we’re just killing time
Like a houseplant bending toward the windowsill’s climb
But if heaven’s a place where the coffee’s always hot
Then call me a saint, ‘cause I’m already caught

**[Chorus]**
Oh, but this is the gospel, the only truth I know:
Not the fireworks, but the slow, slow glow
The way the light comes through the blinds at noon
The way you say my name when you’re half-asleep in June

**[Outro]**
So let the world keep its grand design
I’ll take the dust in the sunbeam, the way it’s mine
The way it’s ours for a second, suspended
Before it scatters again—*perfect, unended*

---
**Notes on the song:**
- **Specificity:** The time (6:17), the chipped mug, the *Times* headline, the radiator—grounding the song in tangible details.
- **Metaphors:** "The steam from the bowl makes a ghost of your face," "a houseplant bending toward the windowsill’s climb."
- **Emotional build:** Starts with quiet observation, peaks in the chorus with a declaration ("this is the gospel"), then resolves with acceptance in the outro.
- **Rhyme scheme:** Mostly AABB or ABAB, with slant rhymes ("moon"/"soon," "place"/"face") for a natural feel.
- **Atmosphere:** Warm, intimate, slightly wistful—like sunlight through blinds.

Would fit on a record between *Phoebe Bridgers* and *Big Thief*, maybe with a sparse guitar arpeggio and a distant harmonium.
right_lyrics:
**"Paper Cups & Porch Lights"**
*(An Indie Folk Ballad for the Quiet Moments)*

*(Verse 1)*
The coffee’s cold but I drink it slow,
Watch the steam rise like a ghost let go.
Neighbor’s dog barks at a passing truck,
While the morning hums—soft as luck.

*(Pre-Chorus)*
And the dishes wait in the sink again,
Like old love letters I can’t throw in.
But the light comes slant through the kitchen blind,
And I swear it’s the kindest thing I’ve ever seen.

*(Chorus)*
Oh, the world’s not made of fireworks bright,
Just paper cups and porch lights.
Dust on the shelf where the records spin,
And the way your hand fits in mine again.
If heaven’s a place, then I think it’s here—
In the quiet, in the near,
In the way the wind chimes sing off-key,
In the ordinary, loving me.

*(Verse 2)*
The bus stops at the end of the street,
A kid presses his face to the glass to see.
His breath makes a cloud, his boots are untied,
And the driver just smiles like the whole world’s wide.

*(Pre-Chorus)*
And the radio plays a song I know,
Half the words lost to the static’s glow.
But the melody sticks like the scent of rain,
Like the way you still laugh at my old jokes again.

*(Chorus)*
Oh, the world’s not made of fireworks bright,
Just paper cups and porch lights.
Dust on the shelf where the records spin,
And the way your hand fits in mine again.
If heaven’s a place, then I think it’s here—
In the quiet, in the near,
In the way the wind chimes sing off-key,
In the ordinary, loving me.

*(Bridge)*
Maybe we’re not the kind they write about,
No grand romance, no shouting out.
But the way you hum while you fold the laundry,
That’s the hymn I’ll take to the grave with me.

*(Outro)*
So let the evenings stretch long and slow,
Like a cat in a sunbeam, half-asleep, half aglow.
And if the end comes soft as a turning page,
I’ll go grateful for the quiet, for the small, for the brave.

*(Whispered, fading)*
For the paper cups… and the porch lights.

---
**Style Notes:**
- **Instrumentation:** Fingerpicked acoustic guitar, soft harmonica, warm upright bass, and a distant, tinny piano (like an old saloon piano heard through a screen door).
- **Vibe:** Think *Phoebe Bridgers* meets *The Weepies*—intimate, wistful, but grounded in gratitude. The kind of song that makes you notice the way light hits your coffee mug.
- **Key Lyric:** *"If heaven’s a place, then I think it’s here—/ In the quiet, in the near"* (a nod to the sacred in the mundane).

Would love to hear how you’d tweak it! Maybe add a verse about a specific memory (e.g., a diner at 3 AM, a thrift-store sweater)?

theme: chasing your dreams in a big city
left_lyrics:
**"SKYSCRAPER HEART"**
*(A glittering anthem about chasing dreams under city lights)*

---

**[Verse 1]**
Midnight train, one-way ticket in my hand
Dreams too loud for a small-town stand
Neon signs like a choir singing *"Go!"*
I packed my fears in a backseat window

**[Pre-Chorus]**
Yeah, they said *"Girl, you’re just a spark"*
But I *feel* like the dark’s just a place to start
Every *"no"* just a detour sign
I’ll rewrite the stars in my own design

**[Chorus]**
Oh, I got a **skyscraper heart**, unshakable fire
Dancing on rooftops, reaching up higher
They can keep their *"what-ifs"*, I’ll take the view
‘Cause the city don’t sleep—and neither do I, *do-do-do*
*Oh-oh-oh*, I’m a satellite, orbit the glow
Every streetlight’s a spotlight when you *know*
You were born for the rush, for the fall and the flight
Yeah, I got a **skyscraper heart** tonight!

**[Verse 2]**
Subway preachers and poets in the rain
Lost my umbrella but kept the pain
Turned it into a bridge, turned it into gold
Now the pavement’s my stage, yeah, the story’s untold

**[Pre-Chorus]**
They said *"Dreamer, slow down, take it easy"*
But the night moves too fast when you’re *hungry*
Every scar’s just a proof I tried
I’ll be the name in the neon lights

**[Chorus]**
Oh, I got a **skyscraper heart**, unshakable fire
Dancing on rooftops, reaching up higher
They can keep their *"what-ifs"*, I’ll take the view
‘Cause the city don’t sleep—and neither do I, *do-do-do*
*Oh-oh-oh*, I’m a satellite, orbit the glow
Every streetlight’s a spotlight when you *know*
You were born for the rush, for the fall and the flight
Yeah, I got a **skyscraper heart** tonight!

**[Bridge]**
*Hey!* Let the sirens sing my anthem
*Hey!* Let the crowds all scream my name in
If I crash? I’ll crash *loud*—
A supernova in the downtown clouds!

**[Final Chorus]**
Oh, I got a **skyscraper heart**, *unbreakable* now
Turned all my *"should’ve beens"* to *"wow"*s
They can keep their *"good luck"*, I’ll take the fight
‘Cause the stars don’t blink in this city tonight!
*Oh-oh-oh*, I’m a wildfire, burning so bright
Every *"no"* just a dare—watch me *ignite*
I was born for the rush, for the fall and the flight
Yeah, I got a **skyscraper heart**—*tonight!*

---
**Why it works:**
- **Hooky Title/Chorus:** "Skyscraper Heart" is visual, aspirational, and singable.
- **Rhyme/Rhythm:** Mix of multi-syllabic rhymes (*"design"/"sign"*) and punchy internal rhymes (*"spark"/"dark"/"start"*).
- **Emotional Arc:** Verse 1 = arrival, Verse 2 = struggle, Bridge = defiance, Final Chorus = triumph.
- **Pop Tropes:** City lights, underdog energy, "rewriting stars" (nod to *The Greatest Showman* but fresher).
- **Melodic Implication:** Short, declarative lines (*"I’m a satellite"*) beg for big, soaring notes; longer lines (*"Every no just a detour sign"*) suggest a rhythmic build.

**Artist Fit:** Imagine this on **Dua Lipa** (disco-pop edge), **Miley Cyrus** (raspy belting), or **Troye Sivan** (glittery synth vibes).
right_lyrics:

*(A Pop Anthem for the Chasers)*

**[Verse 1]**
Midnight train, one-way ticket in my hand,

**Vibe:** Think Dua Lipa’s *"Don’t Start Now"* meets early Katy Perry’s *"Firework"*—glittery, defiant, and made for singing into a hairbrush in front of a skyline view. 🎤✨

Want a specific twist? More rock? More R&B? A rap verse? Let me know!

theme: a love that burns too hot
left_lyrics:
**"BURN THIS LOVE"**

*(A scorching anthem about passion that consumes—where desire and destruction walk hand in hand.)*

---

**[Verse 1]**
Your kiss is kerosene,
A spark, a match, a gasoline dream.
I knew the first time that you touched my skin,
This fire would leave me hollow again.

**[Verse 2]**
We dance in the ashes,
Two fools with a death wish.
Every "I love you" sounds like a lie,
When the smoke in your eyes says goodbye.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
I should run, but I’m drawn to the blaze,
Like a moth to a funeral pyre’s gaze.
One more night, one more scar,
One more chance to fall apart.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, we burn this love,
Like a city in the night.
No survivors, no regrets,
Just the light before we die.
Oh, we burn this love,
Hotter than the devil’s luck.
If heaven won’t take us in,
Then hell can’t give us up.

**[Verse 3]**
You’re the storm, I’m the flood,
A reckless kind of love.
We write our names in the wreckage left,
A graffiti of all the things we’ve wrecked.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
I should run, but I’m drawn to the blaze,
Like a moth to a funeral pyre’s gaze.
One more night, one more scar,
One more chance to fall apart.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, we burn this love,
Like a city in the night.
No survivors, no regrets,
Just the light before we die.
Oh, we burn this love,
Hotter than the devil’s luck.
If heaven won’t take us in,
Then hell can’t give us up.

**[Bridge]**
*(Spoken, over a slow, smoldering build)*
*"They say love’s supposed to save you…*
*But darlin’, we were born to raze it.*
*So strike the match, let the world turn black—*
*At least we’ll go down satisfied."*

**[Guitar Solo]**
*(A searing, blues-infused solo that screams and wails—pure emotional release.)*

**[Final Chorus]**
Oh, we burn this love,
Like a city in the night.
No survivors, no regrets,
Just the light before we die.
Oh, we burn this love,
Hotter than the devil’s luck.
If heaven won’t take us in,
Then hell can’t give us—
*(whispered, over fading feedback)*
**—us up.**

---
**How it hits:**
- **Verses:** Dark, sensual imagery with a sense of inevitability. The rhymes are tight but not forced, giving it a natural, conversational bite.
- **Pre-Chorus:** Builds tension with a confession—*"I should run"*—that we all know he won’t follow through on.
- **Chorus:** **Huge, sing-along, fist-pumping.** The *"Oh, we burn this love"* line is designed to be screamed by a crowd, with the *"hell can’t give us up"* twist adding defiance.
- **Bridge:** A moment of raw, spoken vulnerability before the solo **unleashes chaos.**
- **Ending:** Fades into feedback, leaving the listener breathless—like the aftermath of a fire.

**For the stage:**
- The bridge’s spoken word gives the singer a moment to **lock eyes with the crowd** before the solo explodes.
- The final chorus **drops to a whisper**, letting the audience scream the last line back at the band.
- **Pyro optional but encouraged.** 🔥
right_lyrics:
**"Burning Out the Sun"**
*(A Rock Song About Love That Burns Too Hot)*

**[Verse 1]**
You lit the match, I lit the fuse,
A backdraft love in crimson hues.
One touch and I was up in flames,
Now I’m just ashes calling your name.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
You’re gasoline, I’m a spark,
We’re a wildfire in the dark.
I should run, but I stay—
Addicted to the burn, the ache, the blaze.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, we’re **burning out the sun**,
Two hearts too hot to ever be one.
Every kiss is a warning sign,
But I’ll take the heat one last time.
Oh, we’re **melting steel and bone**,
A love this wild was never meant to hold.
I’ll let it scorch me black and blue—
At least I’ll know I burned for you.

**[Verse 2]**
Your love’s a fever, no cure in sight,
I’m drowning in the devil’s light.
We crash like stars, we collide,
Leaving nothing but the scars inside.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
You’re dynamite, I’m the flame,
We’re a supernova of pain.
I should walk, but I crawl—
Drawn to the wreckage, the heat, the fall.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, we’re **burning out the sun**,
Two hearts too hot to ever be one.
Every kiss is a warning sign,
But I’ll take the heat one last time.
Oh, we’re **melting steel and bone**,
A love this wild was never meant to hold.
I’ll let it scorch me black and blue—
At least I’ll know I burned for you.

**[Bridge]**
*(Guitar solo – raw, wailing, full of fire)*
*(Spoken, over a slow, smoldering riff)*
*"They say love’s supposed to warm you…*
*Ours just turns me to dust."*

**[Outro]**
So strike the match, let’s go down,
In a blaze of glory, no sound.
If love’s a fire, then we’re the kind
That leaves the world **blind**.

*(Final chorus – bigger, grittier, voice cracking with emotion)*
Oh, we’re **burning out the sun**…
*(Guitar feedback fades into silence.)*

---
**Style Notes:**
- **Sound:** Think *The Black Keys* meets *Halestorm*—bluesy, gritty guitars, a driving bassline, and a chorus that *explodes*.
- **Delivery:** Raspy, desperate vocals in the verses, soaring and defiant in the chorus. The bridge should feel like the calm before the final inferno.
- **Theme:** Love as destruction, but also as something so intense it’s worth the ruin. Classic rock tragedy with a modern edge.

Want a specific key or tempo? Or maybe a twist on the lyrics? Let me know—I’ll stoke the fire higher. 🔥🎸

*(A Country Song About Falling for Your Best Friend)*

*(Verse 1)*

Ridin’ dirt roads in my daddy’s old truck again.
You knew my dreams before I ever said ‘em out loud,

Never worked out—guess we’re both stubborn.
But when you cry on my shoulder after midnight,
And I’m the one who makes it right,
I swear the air gets thick like summer rain,
And I forget we’re just supposed to stay the same.

*(Chorus)*
Oh, we’re more than just friends, but you don’t know,
I’m drownin’ in the things I can’t let show.
Every campfire, every old love song,
Every time you sing along—I’m gone.
One of these days, I’ll find the courage true,
But till then, I’ll just ache for you.

*(Bridge)*
Maybe it’s the way you steal my fries,
Or how you’re first to call when trouble rises.
Maybe it’s the way you fit right in my arms,
Like you were built to keep me warm.
Lord, I’m scared to ruin what we got,
But darlin’, I’m already tied in knots.

*(Outro)*
So here’s to the friend who don’t see my heart,
To the love that’s been here right from the start.
If I ever get brave, I’ll tell you true—
But till then, I’ll just love you…
Like the best friend I’m supposed to be,
While you’re the only one I’ll ever need.

*(Soft guitar fade-out…)*

---
Hope you like it! This one’s got all the classic country tropes—unspoken love, small-town nostalgia, and that bittersweet ache of wanting more but being too scared to risk it. Perfect for a bonfire or a lonely highway drive. 🎸💔

right_lyrics:
**"Ocean Between Us"**
*(A Pop Ballad About Longing & Distance)*

**[Verse 1]**
I trace your name on my windowpane,
The rain just falls like tears again.
Your voice still plays on my old voicemail,
But it’s just static now—my heart’s a sail,
Lost in the waves between us.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
I swear I see you in the city lights,
A shadow moves just like your stride.
But when I blink, you disappear,
And I’m left here—
Wishing you were near.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, there’s an ocean between us,
A thousand miles of blue.
Every night I’m reaching,
But my hands can’t hold you.
Time zones keep us silent,
The sunrise steals my view.
If love’s a bridge, then why does it feel
Like I’m drowning in the space between me and you?

**[Verse 2]**
Your hoodie still smells like your cologne,
I wear it when I’m missing home.
The stars don’t shine the same alone,
And my bed’s too wide, my heart’s a phone—
Always on, but no reply.

**[Pre-Chorus]**
I count the days on my calendar,
Each one’s a scar that won’t heal over.
If I could fold the map in half,
I’d run so fast—
Just to crash into your arms.

**[Chorus]**
Oh, there’s an ocean between us,
A thousand miles of blue.
Every night I’m reaching,
But my hands can’t hold you.
Time zones keep us silent,
The sunrise steals my view.
If love’s a bridge, then why does it feel
Like I’m drowning in the space between me and you?

**[Bridge]**
Maybe one day, the tides will turn,
And the miles will finally burn.
Till then, I’ll send my love in waves,
Hoping you’re still brave enough to stay…
(Stay…)

**[Outro]**
Oh, there’s an ocean between us,
But I’m swimming through.
Even if the current pulls me under,
I’ll find my way back to you.
Yeah, there’s an ocean between us—
But darling, I’m coming home to you.

---
**Vibe:** Think *The Chainsmokers meets Lewis Capaldi*—a mix of electronic-pop production with raw, emotional vocals. The bridge could build with a soaring synth or a simple, stripped-down piano for maximum feels.
